@@ -19,7 +19,7 @@
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.3.4","title":"Decision modes: E2E coverage (deny vs warn vs log-only behavior)","description":"## Objective

Add end-to-end tests proving decision modes behave correctly in the hook protocol.

## Why

Decision mode bugs are extremely user-visible:

- WARN must allow (no stdout JSON deny) but still provide stderr output.
- LOG-ONLY must be silent (no stdout, no stderr) but may emit structured logs.

## Test cases

- Configure a non-catastrophic rule to WARN and verify:
  - exit allows
  - stderr contains a warning
  - stdout is empty

- Configure a rule to LOG-ONLY and verify:
  - stdout empty
  - stderr empty

- Verify catastrophic rules remain DENY by default.

## Logging requirements

- Print captured stdout/stderr on failure (truncated).
- Print which config/policy settings were active.

## Acceptance criteria

- Tests would catch accidental protocol regressions (e.g., WARN emitting JSON deny).

## Dependencies

- `git_safety_guard-1gt.3` decision modes implementation
- `git_safety_guard-99e.3.1` shared evaluator
","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:24:26.009028742-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T22:24:26.009028742-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.3.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:24:36.855424505-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.3.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-99e.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:24:54.034020082-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.4","title":"Structured logging for allow/deny decisions (optional redaction)","description":"## Objective

Improve operational visibility without compromising privacy by offering structured logs for decisions.

## Background

`dcg` already supports a simple log file for blocked commands. As we add more sophisticated analysis (execution context, heredocs, AST), we need logs that can support:

- diagnosing false positives
- tuning config
- measuring performance impact

## Proposed logging fields

At minimum (on deny/warn/log-only events):

- timestamp
- mode (deny/warn/log-only)
- raw command (optionally redacted)
- normalized command
- pack_id
- pattern_name
- reason
- execution context summary (if available)
- elapsed time (optional)

## Redaction strategy

- Provide a config option to redact arguments beyond a threshold or redact quoted strings.
- Default behavior should be conservative (log only for deny events).

## Acceptance criteria

- Logs are well-formed and append-only.
- A `--json` log format exists (optional).
- Unit tests verify logging output format.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.5","title":"Suggestions DB: safe alternatives + how-to-allow guidance","description":"## Objective

Make `dcg` helpful, not just restrictive, by attaching curated suggestions to blocked rules.

When a command is blocked (or warned), the user should immediately see:

- what happened (stable rule id)
- why it's risky
- safe alternatives / preview steps
- how to allow safely (rule-id allowlist command)

## Key idea

Maintain a small, explicit "suggestions database" keyed by stable rule id:

- `rule_id = "pack_id:pattern_name"`

Each rule can have 0..N suggestions:

- `preview_first` (e.g., `git clean -n`, `docker system df`)
- `safer_alternative` (e.g., `--force-with-lease`)
- `workflow_fix` (e.g., `git stash` first)
- `allow_safely` (copy/paste allowlist command)
- `docs` (README anchor)

## Why this is worth doing

- Reduces "rage disable" moments.
- Teaches users safer habits.
- Makes false positives much cheaper to resolve.

## Constraints

- Suggestions must be non-prescriptive when environment-specific.
- Suggestions must be safe-by-default (no destructive commands suggested).
- Hook output must remain bounded in size.

## Acceptance criteria

- For core rules, deny output includes at least one actionable suggestion.
- Explain mode includes suggestions in all formats.
- Suggestions are deterministic and test-covered.

-{"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.5.1","title":"Suggestions: data model + registry keyed by rule id","description":"## Objective

Define the suggestion data model and implement a registry lookup by stable rule id.

## Background

When DCG blocks a command, users need actionable guidance:
- What safer alternatives exist?
- How can they preview the effect first?
- How can they allowlist if intentional?

A well-designed suggestion system improves UX and reduces frustration.

## SuggestionKind Enum

```rust
/// Type of suggestion to help the user
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
pub enum SuggestionKind {
    /// "Run this first to preview the effect"
    /// e.g., "Run `git diff` before `git reset --hard`"
    PreviewFirst,

    /// "Use this safer alternative instead"
    /// e.g., "Use `git reset --soft` or `--mixed` instead of `--hard`"
    SaferAlternative,

    /// "Fix your workflow to avoid this situation"
    /// e.g., "Commit your changes before resetting"
    WorkflowFix,

    /// "Read the documentation for more context"
    /// e.g., "See: https://git-scm.com/docs/git-reset"
    Documentation,

    /// "How to allowlist this specific rule"
    /// e.g., "To allow: `dcg allow core.git:reset-hard --reason '...'`"
    AllowSafely,
}
```

## Suggestion Struct

```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct Suggestion {
    /// Type of suggestion
    pub kind: SuggestionKind,

    /// Human-readable suggestion text
    pub text: String,

    /// Optional command the user can copy/paste
    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    pub command: Option<String>,

    /// Optional URL for documentation
    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    pub url: Option<String>,
}
```

## Registry Implementation

```rust
use std::sync::LazyLock;
use std::collections::HashMap;

/// Registry of suggestions keyed by rule_id (e.g., "core.git:reset-hard")
static SUGGESTION_REGISTRY: LazyLock<HashMap<&'static str, Vec<Suggestion>>> =
    LazyLock::new(|| {
        let mut m = HashMap::new();

        m.insert("core.git:reset-hard", vec![
            Suggestion {
                kind: SuggestionKind::PreviewFirst,
                text: "Run `git diff` and `git status` to see what would be lost".into(),
                command: Some("git diff && git status".into()),
                url: None,
            },
            Suggestion {
                kind: SuggestionKind::SaferAlternative,
                text: "Use `git reset --soft` or `--mixed` to preserve changes".into(),
                command: Some("git reset --soft HEAD~1".into()),
                url: None,
            },
            Suggestion {
                kind: SuggestionKind::WorkflowFix,
                text: "Consider using `git stash` to save changes temporarily".into(),
                command: Some("git stash".into()),
                url: None,
            },
        ]);

        // ... more rules
        m
    });

/// Look up suggestions for a rule
pub fn get_suggestions(rule_id: &str) -> Option<&'static [Suggestion]> {
    SUGGESTION_REGISTRY.get(rule_id).map(|v| v.as_slice())
}
```

## Output Formatting

### Hook Deny Output (stderr)
Show only top 1-3 suggestions to keep output bounded:

```
╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║  ⚠️  BLOCKED: git reset --hard HEAD~5                        ║
╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
║  Rule: core.git:reset-hard                                   ║
║  Reason: Hard reset can permanently lose uncommitted changes ║
╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
║  💡 Try instead:                                             ║
║  • Run `git diff` first to see what would be lost            ║
║  • Use `git reset --soft` to preserve changes                ║
╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
```

### Explain Mode (JSON)
Include all suggestions in structured format:

```json
{
  "decision": "deny",
  "rule_id": "core.git:reset-hard",
  "reason": "Hard reset can permanently lose uncommitted changes",
  "suggestions": [
    {
      "kind": "preview_first",
      "text": "Run `git diff` and `git status` to see what would be lost",
      "command": "git diff && git status"
    },
    {
      "kind": "safer_alternative",
      "text": "Use `git reset --soft` or `--mixed` to preserve changes",
      "command": "git reset --soft HEAD~1"
    }
  ]
}
```

## Requirements

- Registry lookup is O(1) via HashMap
- Zero allocations on lookup (return static references)
- Suggestions are stable and deterministic (iteration order preserved)
- JSON output uses snake_case for kind values

## Acceptance Criteria

- Registry lookup is fast and allocation-light.
- JSON output for explain includes suggestions in a stable order.
- All SuggestionKind variants are used appropriately across rules.

## Tests

- Unit tests for registry lookup and ordering.
- Snapshot tests for JSON output format.
- Coverage test: assert all core pack rules have at least one suggestion.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:06:31.606222639-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T21:22:28.669910234-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.5.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:07:01.11809851-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.5.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-99e.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:22:31.439334729-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

║  Reason: Hard reset can permanently lose uncommitted changes ║

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.5.2","title":"Suggestions: populate core.git + core.filesystem","description":"## Objective

Populate suggestions for the highest-traffic and highest-impact rules:

- `core.git:*`
- `core.filesystem:*`

## What to include

For each rule:

- a short "preview first" command when applicable
- a safer alternative when one exists
- the recommended allowlist command (rule-id based)

Examples (conceptual):

- `core.git:reset-hard`:
  - suggest `git stash` / `git diff` / `git reset --soft|--mixed`
- `core.git:push-force-*`:
  - suggest `--force-with-lease`
- `core.filesystem:rm-rf-*`:
  - suggest verifying path and using safer deletion flows when possible

## Acceptance criteria

- Every core rule has at least one suggestion.
- Suggestions are accurate and generic (no environment assumptions).

## Tests

- A "coverage" test that asserts all core destructive patterns have at least 1 suggestion.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:06:36.697958943-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T21:06:36.697958943-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.5.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:07:06.209112022-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.5.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:22:36.533341338-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.5.3","title":"Suggestions: populate high-signal non-core packs (docker/k8s/db)","description":"## Objective

Populate suggestions for the packs most likely to appear in real dev workflows:

- Docker
- kubectl
- databases (psql/mysql/redis)

## Guidance

Prefer "preview" suggestions:

- docker: `docker system df` before `docker system prune`
- kubectl: `--dry-run=client` / `kubectl diff`
- SQL: backup / transaction guidance (keep generic)

## Acceptance criteria

- High-signal destructive patterns in these packs have suggestions.
- Suggestions are concise and safe.
","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### Tests
- Coverage belongs in `git_safety_guard-1gt.5.5`:
  - deny output includes suggestion(s) for top rules in docker/k8s/db packs
  - suggestions are keyed by stable rule ID and remain deterministic","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:06:41.789029161-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T23:26:02.688030654-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.5.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:07:11.302023318-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.5.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:22:41.631324095-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.5.4","title":"Integrate suggestions into deny output and explain mode","description":"## Objective

Make suggestions visible where they matter:

- hook deny output (stderr) includes the top suggestion(s)
- `dcg explain` includes all suggestions (pretty/json/compact)

This task is also where we implement the **guided learning workflow** (hook-safe, non-interactive):

- on block, print copy/pasteable next steps (`dcg explain ...`, `dcg allowlist add ...`)
- no prompts; the hook must never wait for user input

## Requirements

### Hook deny output

- Must remain bounded; show only top 1–3 suggestions.
- Must include:
  - stable rule id (`pack_id:pattern_name`)
  - a short reason
  - at least one safe alternative suggestion (when available)
  - a copy/paste allowlist command example, e.g.:
    - `dcg allowlist add <rule_id> --reason "..." --project`
  - a copy/paste explain command:
    - `dcg explain "<command>"`

### Explain output

- Show all suggestions.
- JSON includes suggestions as structured objects.

## Acceptance criteria

- Deny output shows stable rule id + at least one suggestion.
- Deny output includes copy/paste learning commands (explain + allowlist add).
- Explain mode shows suggestions in all formats.

## Tests

- Snapshot tests for deny output (stderr) for a few core rules.
- Explain output tests include suggestions list.
- Regression test: deny output stays under a size cap even if a rule has many suggestions.
","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### Determinism
- Suggestion ordering must be deterministic:
  - sort by severity then stable rule ID
  - cap list with a stable tie-breaker
- Output formatting must not depend on hash iteration order.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:06:46.881705224-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T23:26:16.266936339-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.5.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:07:16.392198141-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.5.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:22:46.724344957-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.5.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:22:51.819635023-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.5.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:22:56.914257712-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.5.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:23:02.00798218-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
